location: The collapsed entrance of the Ashen Barrow
scene: Torchlight gutters against damp stone. A goblin sentry, Snik, bars the only passage deeper into the barrow, blade already drawn. Your party: Brother Aldric, a road-worn cleric, and Wisp, a half-elf mage whose spellbook is nearly spent.

barrow_entrance.location: The collapsed entrance of the Ashen Barrow
barrow_entrance.scene: Torchlight gutters against damp stone. A goblin sentry, Snik, bars the only passage deeper into the barrow, blade already drawn. Your party: Brother Aldric, a road-worn cleric, and Wisp, a half-elf mage whose spellbook is nearly spent.

log: [turn 1] combat started round 1, order: ['aldric', 'snik', 'wisp'], first: aldric

transcript.text: move forward towards the goblin

Aldric's boots scrape against the damp stone as he advances, torch flame whipping sideways in the sudden draft. Snik bares a mouthful of jagged teeth, shortsword leveled at chest height, yellow eyes darting between the cleric and the pale, swaying figure of Wisp behind him — sensing weakness, calculating odds.
swing the mace

Aldric's mace arcs in a powerful overhead swing, cracking squarely against Snik's collarbone with a satisfying crunch. The goblin staggers back a step, spitting a curse in his raspy tongue — hurt, but far from finished, his yellow eyes now blazing with fury.

Snarling with rage, Snik lunges forward and drives his shortsword at Aldric's gut — but the blade skitters harmlessly off the cleric's armour, sparks dancing in the torchlight as the goblin's fury outpaces his aim.
use dagger
Wisp lunges forward on trembling legs, driving the dagger at Snik with desperate urgency — but the blade swings wide, slicing nothing but cold air as the goblin sidesteps with contemptuous ease.
use mace

Aldric's mace comes down with the full weight of his devotion behind it, crashing against Snik's skull with a brutal crack that echoes off the barrow's stone walls. The goblin's knees buckle, a strangled yelp escaping his twisted lips as he staggers, blood matting the coarse fur at his temple — still standing, barely, but the arrogance has drained clean from those yellow eyes.

Snik snarls and thrusts his shortsword at Aldric in a wild, desperate lunge — but the cleric's armour turns the blade aside with a dull scrape of metal on metal, and the goblin's momentum carries him stumbling half a step forward, leaving him dangerously off-balance.
use dagger

Wisp's dagger finds its mark this time — the blade punches into Snik's side with a wet, satisfying *thwack*, drawing a high-pitched shriek from the goblin. It's a small wound on a surprisingly resilient creature, but Wisp's eyes gleam with grim satisfaction as dark blood wells around the hilt's edge.
use mace

Aldric brings the mace down in a thunderous arc, the critical blow landing square on Snik's crown with a resounding crack — enough force to floor any ordinary foe. Yet the goblin merely staggers, shakes the blood from his eyes, and grins through broken teeth, something unnatural keeping him upright when he should be crumpled on the cold stone floor.

Snik lunges at Aldric with a furious, snarling thrust of his shortsword, but the cleric holds his ground and the blade glances off the chainmail with a hollow clang — the goblin's wounded arm betraying him at the last moment, his strike carrying all rage and no precision.
dagger

Wisp's dagger stabs forward in a desperate, shaking thrust, but there is nothing left in her spent limbs — the blade scrapes uselessly against Snik's armour and she lurches sideways, barely catching herself against the barrow wall, breath coming in ragged, rattling gasps.
mace

Aldric drives the mace into Snik's ribs with a focused, powerful blow — bone cracks and the goblin lets out a sharp, wheezing grunt, lurching backward. Seven points of punishment land clean, yet Snik somehow remains upright, hunched and bleeding, that unnatural vitality still burning in his hateful yellow eyes.

Snik's shortsword finds a gap between Aldric's pauldron and gorget, punching a shallow but stinging wound into the cleric's shoulder — four points of hot, piercing pain that draw a sharp hiss through Aldric's clenched teeth. The goblin wrenches the blade free with a wet rasp and bares his broken-toothed grin, emboldened by the first blood he's drawn in rounds.
dagger

Wisp summons the last dregs of her will and drives the dagger into Snik's thigh — a shallow, desperate jab that draws blood and a snarl from the goblin, but does precious little to slow the unnatural creature. She slumps back against the cold barrow wall, knuckles white around the hilt, her ragged breathing the only sound she can manage.
mace

Aldric brings the mace down in a savage, perfectly-timed arc, the critical blow crunching into Snik's jaw with a spray of black ichor and shattered teeth — a hit that would drop any mortal creature stone dead. Yet the goblin merely rolls with the impact, spitting a mouthful of ruin onto the barrow floor, and straightens back up with those hateful yellow eyes burning brighter than ever, utterly unmoved by punishment that defies all natural law.

Snik snarls and drives his shortsword at Aldric's throat in a wild, hatred-fuelled lunge — but the cleric steps inside the blow with practiced ease, the blade skittering harmlessly off his chainmail with a hollow scrape, Snik's broken jaw robbing the strike of any real fury.
swing the dagger

Wisp's trembling arm swings the dagger in a wild, desperate arc — but her body is nearly spent, and the blade cuts nothing but cold barrow air, skittering off the stone wall in a shower of sparks. Snik doesn't even flinch, those yellow eyes sliding to her with something almost like contempt.
swing mace

Snik lunges at Aldric with a guttural shriek, shortsword slashing in a vicious diagonal — but Aldric catches the blow on his forearm, the blade skittering off the chainmail with a hollow ring, the goblin's broken body failing to land the killing stroke it so desperately wants.

Snik's shortsword darts forward with unnatural speed, finding the same wound in Aldric's ribs he opened moments ago — a cruel, precise thrust that drives four points of piercing agony deep into the cleric's side, staggering him back a half-step against the barrow wall. Aldric's breath comes in short, ragged pulls now, blood darkening the links of his chainmail, but his grip on the mace does not waver.
dagger

Wisp's dagger jabs forward in a last, feeble thrust, but her arm shakes so violently the blade scrapes nothing but cold stone at Snik's feet — a pitiful swing that draws a wet, gurgling laugh from the goblin's shattered jaw. She is nearly spent, clinging to consciousness by a thread, and Snik knows it.
mace

Aldric swings the mace in a punishing overhead arc, but exhaustion and the blood seeping from his side betray him — the blow sails wide, glancing off the barrow's stone doorframe with a dull clang that shudders up his arm and leaves Snik untouched, the goblin ducking beneath it with an almost contemptuous ease.

Snik wheels away from Aldric and lunges at Wisp with a feral shriek, shortsword driving straight for her heart — but the little wizard stumbles sideways on trembling legs and the blade hisses past her ear, close enough to slice a lock of singed hair from her temple, the killing stroke denied by sheer, desperate luck.
dagger

With a last, desperate surge of will, Wisp drives the dagger upward into the gap beneath Snik's leather jerkin — the blade bites true, and the goblin lets out a sharp, wet yelp, staggering back a half-step with dark blood welling between his fingers as he clutches his ribs. It is a small wound on a creature that has weathered far worse, but Wisp stands a little straighter, the ghost of triumph flickering in her exhausted eyes.
mace

Aldric's mace crashes down onto Snik's shoulder with a bone-cracking thud, the goblin's arm buckling under the impact as he's driven a stumbling step sideways into the barrow wall — a grunt of pain tears from his ruined jaw, dark blood spattering the ancient stonework. But Snik rights himself with savage stubbornness, yellow eyes blazing, shortsword still clutched in a white-knuckled fist.

Snik wheels on Wisp with murder in his eyes, shortsword slashing in a vicious horizontal arc aimed at her throat — but the blade cuts only empty air as Wisp throws herself backward against the barrow wall, the steel kissing nothing but cold shadow an inch from her chin.
dagger

Wisp's blade finds the meat of Snik's thigh with a precise, desperate thrust, and the goblin's howl splits the barrow air like a cracked bell — dark blood sheets down his leg as he staggers, teeth bared in a rictus of rage and pain. He does not fall. But for one breathless moment, the monster looks almost afraid.
mace

Snik lunges at Wisp with a shriek of animal rage, shortsword aimed to gut her where she stands — the blade connects, tearing across her side in a shallow but vicious slash that drops her to one knee with a ragged gasp. The torchlight wavers as she crumples against the cold stone, fingers pressed to the wound, her breath coming in thin, desperate gulps.

Snik plants a boot on the edge of Wisp's crumpled form and throws back his head in a rattling, triumphant shriek, shortsword raised toward the barrow ceiling — a victor's howl that echoes off the ancient stones and fills the passage with something cold and gloating.

Wisp lies deathly still, her lips parted around a breath that barely comes — somewhere in the darkness behind her eyes she fights, but the darkness pushes back, and the first tremor of defeat moves through her like a cold tide; one failure, and the silence grows heavier.
mace

Aldric's mace swings wide — a desperate, grief-fuelled arc that clips the barrow wall with a hollow clang and sends sparks skittering across the stone. The goblin doesn't even flinch, already turning his yellow gaze back down to Wisp's crumpled form with something like hunger in his eyes. Aldric stands over her, mace raised, chest heaving — the only thing between her and the blade.

Snik feints low and drives his shortsword at Aldric's ribs, but the cleric's armour turns the blade with a screech of steel on steel — the blow glances harmlessly away, and for a single heartbeat Snik is overextended, snarling in frustration just inches from Aldric's face.

On the cold stone floor, Wisp shudders once — a rattling, involuntary tremor that runs through her whole body — and then goes terribly still again, the darkness behind her eyes deepening like ink dropped in water. Two failures now. The thread of her life grows thinner, fraying at the edges, and the barrow seems to lean in closer as if it has already begun to claim her.
mace

Snik lunges forward with terrifying speed, driving his shortsword in a brutal thrust at Aldric's chest — the blade punches through a gap in the cleric's armour and bites deep, and Aldric staggers backward with a choked cry, one hand flying to the wound as hot blood soaks through his vestments. He catches himself against the barrow wall, mace still clutched in a trembling fist, teeth gritted against the pain — but his eyes stay on Snik, burning with desperate, furious resolve.

Snik plants his boot on the barrow floor and stands tall over the carnage, shortsword dripping, his rattling shriek of triumph bouncing off the ancient stones — he turns his yellow eyes from the kneeling Aldric to the still form of Wisp with an expression of absolute, hollow contempt.

On the cold floor, Wisp's body shudders once — the faintest flutter of her lashes, a breath that never quite arrives — and then she is still, truly still, the light behind her eyes gone out like a snuffed candle; the barrow claims her, and the silence that follows is the loudest thing Aldric has ever heard.
mace

Snik moves with savage, unhurried precision — the shortsword coming in low and fast, a butcher's stroke aimed at Aldric's legs — and the blade finds its mark with a wet, sickening thud that buckles the cleric's knee and drops him hard against the cold stone floor. Aldric's mace clatters from his fingers, rolling away into the dark. He lies there in the torchlight, breath rattling, one hand outstretched toward Wisp's still form as the shadows press in from every corner of the barrow.

Snik straightens to his full, wiry height above Aldric's crumpled form, chest heaving, yellow eyes bright with contempt — he turns a slow circle in the torchlight as if daring the barrow itself to produce another challenger, shortsword trailing a thin line of blood across the ancient stones.
mace

From his knees, Aldric drags every last scrap of will he has left and swings his mace in a wild, desperate arc — the head connects with Snik's shin with a satisfying crack of iron on bone, and the goblin lets out a shriek of pain and hops backward, clutching his leg, yellow eyes blazing with sudden, furious surprise. It isn't enough to end him — but it's enough to tell Snik that Aldric is still in the fight, still breathing, still dangerous — a wounded animal with nothing left to lose.

Snik snarls through the pain in his shin and drives his shortsword down in a savage overhead thrust, catching Aldric across the shoulder and raking the blade along his collarbone with a wet, tearing sound — Aldric slumps against the barrow wall, blood soaking dark through his vestments, fingers still wrapped around his mace with the last stubborn grip of a man who refuses to let go.
